evals.prompt: I want to create a custom agent for my .NET project that acts as a code reviewer. It should only be able to read files and search the codebase — not edit anything. It should ask clarifying questions when needed and write its findings to memory so other agents can read them. It should never show up in the agents dropdown since it'll always be called by our orchestrator agent.
evals.expected_output: A .agent.md file placed in .github/agents/ with: description, user-invocable: false, tools restricted to ['search', 'read', 'vscode/memory', 'vscode/askQuestions'], and a body that describes the review protocol and instructs the agent to write findings to /memories/session/review.md
assertions: user-invocable: false is set in frontmatter (not hidden: true or any other property)
Tools list uses category set names (search, read) not individual VS Code API tool names (read_file, grep_search, etc.)
vscode/memory appears in the tools list
vscode/askQuestions appears in the tools list
Agent name uses PascalCase (e.g., CodeReviewer not code-reviewer)
Body contains instructions to write findings to a memory path (e.g., /memories/session/review.md)

evals.prompt: I need an agent that automates browser tasks using a Playwright MCP server. The agent should: (1) use a Playwright MCP server defined as command: npx, args: ['@playwright/mcp'], (2) only be invokable by other agents — it should never appear in the agents dropdown, (3) use the browser tool category for automation. It should navigate to URLs, take screenshots, fill forms, and click elements as instructed.
evals.expected_output: A .agent.md file with: user-invocable: false, tools: ['browser'] or including browser, mcp-servers: section with a playwright entry using command: npx and args: ['@playwright/mcp'], and a body describing browser automation capabilities
assertions: mcp-servers: section is present in the frontmatter
The MCP server entry uses command: npx and args containing @playwright/mcp
user-invocable: false is set (agent is not user-selectable)
browser tool category or equivalent browser tools are listed in tools
No edit or execute tools appear in the tools list (read-only + browser only)

evals.prompt: I need an agent that runs a full security audit on our codebase. It should search for hardcoded secrets, check for SQL injection risks, look for missing authorization attributes, and check that all external URLs are validated. It should be user-selectable so I can invoke it directly. It should use a powerful model — Claude Sonnet 4.6. It should NOT be able to modify any files. At the end it should present a structured report with findings grouped by severity.
evals.expected_output: A security-auditor.agent.md or similar in .github/agents/ with: description suitable for user selection, user-invocable: true (or omitted), model: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (copilot), tools: ['search', 'read'] (no edit/execute), and a body that describes the audit protocol, what to check, and the report format
assertions: model: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (copilot) is set exactly (qualified vendor format, not claude-sonnet-4-6)
Tools list uses category set names (search, read) not individual tool names (read_file, grep_search, etc.)
No edit or execute tools are listed
user-invocable: true or omitted (agent is user-selectable)
Body documents at least three of: secrets scan, SQL injection, authorization check, URL validation
